name: Bootstrapping the Zero Curve
short_description: Sequentially extract zero-coupon rates from observed coupon bond prices to build the foundational discount curve used across all fixed income pricing.
key_insight: Each successive bond maturity reveals one new zero rate, building the curve sequentially from short to long maturities.

name: Nelson-Siegel Model
short_description: A parsimonious parametric model that captures the level, slope, and curvature of the yield curve with just four parameters, widely used by central banks.
key_insight: Three factors (level, slope, curvature) explain >95% of yield curve variation, enabling smooth interpolation and extrapolation.

name: Vasicek Model
short_description: A pioneering mean-reverting short rate model with an analytical bond pricing formula. Captures the tendency of rates to revert to a long-run equilibrium level.
key_insight: Mean reversion keeps rates bounded around equilibrium, though the model allows negative rates which can be a feature or limitation.

name: Cox-Ingersoll-Ross Model
short_description: Extends the Vasicek framework with a square-root diffusion process that ensures interest rates remain strictly positive while preserving analytical tractability.
key_insight: The sqrt(r) volatility term naturally prevents negative rates and creates heteroskedastic dynamics where vol scales with rate level.

name: Hull-White Model
short_description: A no-arbitrage extension of Vasicek that perfectly fits the current term structure by introducing time-varying drift, making it the workhorse for derivative pricing.
key_insight: Time-dependent theta(t) calibrates exactly to the observed yield curve, eliminating arbitrage from day one of the model.

name: Heath-Jarrow-Morton Framework
short_description: A general no-arbitrage framework that models the evolution of the entire forward rate curve simultaneously, unifying all term structure models under one roof.
key_insight: The drift is fully determined by the volatility function under no-arbitrage, meaning you only need to specify volatility to define the entire model.

name: LIBOR Market Model (BGM)
short_description: Models discrete forward LIBOR rates directly under their own forward measure, producing market-consistent prices for caps and swaptions out of the box.
key_insight: By modeling observable market rates (not abstract short rates), calibration to cap/floor vols is direct and the model speaks the language of traders.

name: Black Model (Black-76)
short_description: Adapts the Black-Scholes framework to price options on forwards and futures, serving as the market standard for quoting interest rate option volatilities.
key_insight: The model provides a common volatility language for the market. Even when rates aren't log-normal, Black vol serves as the universal quoting convention.

name: SABR Model
short_description: A stochastic volatility model that captures the smile/skew in interest rate option markets through correlated dynamics between the forward rate and its volatility.
key_insight: Four parameters (alpha, beta, rho, nu) capture the entire vol surface dynamics, and the closed-form approximation makes calibration lightning fast.

name: Merton Structural Model
short_description: Models a firm's equity as a call option on its assets, linking credit risk directly to the firm's capital structure and asset volatility through option pricing theory.
formula: E = V \cdot N(d_1) - D e^{-rT} N(d_2)
key_insight: Default is endogenous: it happens when asset value falls below the debt barrier, connecting equity markets directly to credit risk.

name: Hazard Rate (Reduced Form) Model
short_description: Models default as a surprise event governed by a stochastic intensity process, enabling direct calibration to market-observed CDS spreads and survival probabilities.
key_insight: Default arrives as a Poisson surprise with intensity lambda, making the model agnostic about why default happens and focused on when.

name: Prepayment Model (CPR)
short_description: Models the conditional prepayment rate of mortgage pools as a function of interest rate incentives, borrower demographics, and seasoning to value mortgage-backed securities.
key_insight: Prepayment is a homeowner's free option: they refinance when rates drop, creating negative convexity that dominates MBS risk.

name: Option-Adjusted Spread (OAS)
short_description: Decomposes the spread of bonds with embedded options into option cost and residual credit/liquidity compensation by simulating cash flows across interest rate paths.
key_insight: OAS strips out the option cost to reveal pure credit/liquidity compensation, enabling apples-to-apples comparison across bonds with different embedded options.

name: Fisher Equation
short_description: The foundational identity linking nominal rates, real rates, and expected inflation, providing the theoretical bridge between nominal and inflation-linked bond markets.
key_insight: Nominal rates embed both real compensation and inflation expectations, and decomposing them reveals the market's forward-looking inflation view.

name: Breakeven Inflation
short_description: The market-implied inflation rate derived from the spread between nominal and inflation-linked bonds, reflecting the inflation rate at which TIPS and nominals break even.
key_insight: Breakeven = pure inflation expectation + risk premium + liquidity premium. Decomposing it reveals what the market truly expects vs demands for bearing inflation risk.
